skill.name: superpowers-lab
skill.description: Lab environment for experimenting with Claude AI capabilities
skill.summary: A documentation skill providing guidance for setting up and using a lab environment to explore and test Claude AI superpowers and advanced features.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Security audit found no actual vulnerabilities. Static analyzer flagged 4 patterns that are all false positives: (1) URLs at lines 4 and 22 are documentation links to GitHub repositories, not executable network requests; (2) Lines 3 and 20 contain prose text describing the skill, not cryptographic code. The skill consists entirely of markdown documentation with no executable code, no network operations, no file system access, and no external command execution.

content.user_title: Set up your Claude AI lab environment
content.value_statement: Need a safe space to experiment with Claude AI capabilities without risk? This skill provides documentation and patterns for creating a controlled lab environment to test and explore advanced Claude features.
actual_capabilities: Provides documentation structure for lab environment setup
Explains when and how to use the lab environment for AI experimentation
Links to source repository for additional resources
Guides users through safe AI capability testing workflows
limitations: Documentation only - does not execute any code or commands
Requires manual setup of lab environment by user
No automated testing or validation features included
External repository link required for full implementation details

use_cases.title: AI Developer Testing
use_cases.description: Set up a controlled environment to test new Claude AI capabilities before production deployment

use_cases.title: Prompt Engineering Research
use_cases.description: Create a safe space to experiment with different prompt patterns and observe Claude behavior

use_cases.title: Team Training
use_cases.description: Establish a learning environment for teams to explore Claude features without affecting production systems

prompt_templates.title: Basic Lab Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me set up a basic lab environment for testing Claude AI capabilities. What directories and files should I create?
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setting up first lab environment

prompt_templates.title: Experiment Design
prompt_templates.prompt: I want to test how Claude handles complex multi-step reasoning tasks. Design an experiment structure for my lab environment.

prompt_templates.title: Safety Boundaries
prompt_templates.prompt: What safety boundaries should I implement in my Claude lab environment to prevent unintended consequences during experimentation?
prompt_templates.scenario: User concerned about safe experimentation practices

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Feature Testing
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a comprehensive testing framework for evaluating Claude Code integration capabilities in isolated lab conditions.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user building systematic test suite

output_examples.input: How do I organize my lab environment for Claude experiments?
output_examples.output: A structured directory layout with separate folders for prompts, outputs, configurations, and test results, each with clear naming conventions and documentation templates.

output_examples.input: What should I track during Claude capability testing?
output_examples.output: Key metrics including response accuracy, latency, token usage, error rates, and qualitative observations about behavior patterns across different prompt variations.

best_practices: Always isolate lab experiments from production systems using separate directories and configurations
Document all experiments with clear descriptions of inputs, expected outputs, and actual results
Establish clear success criteria before beginning any capability testing session
anti_patterns: Testing unvalidated prompts directly in production environments
Failing to log experiment parameters and results for reproducibility
Ignoring rate limits and usage quotas when running automated tests

faq.question: What is a Claude AI lab environment?
faq.answer: A lab environment is a controlled, isolated setup where you can safely experiment with Claude AI capabilities without affecting production systems or real users.

faq.question: Do I need special tools to use this skill?
faq.answer: No special tools are required. This skill provides documentation guidance. You only need a standard development environment and access to Claude AI.

faq.question: Can I use this skill for production testing?
faq.answer: This skill is designed for lab and experimentation purposes. For production testing, you should implement additional safety measures and validation processes.

faq.question: Is my experiment data stored or shared?
faq.answer: This skill is documentation only and does not store or transmit any data. All experiments and their results remain entirely on your local system.

faq.question: How do I get started with the lab environment?
faq.answer: Start by creating a dedicated directory for your lab, then use the prompt templates to guide your initial setup and first experiments with Claude.

faq.question: What if I encounter unexpected Claude behavior?
faq.answer: Document the behavior thoroughly including exact prompts used, then analyze patterns. The lab environment is specifically designed for discovering and understanding such behaviors safely.
